channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: FEMA launches review of migrant shelter aid, suggesting smuggling laws were violated
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration has launched a review of organizations that provide temporary housing and other aid to migrants, suggesting they may have violated a law used to prosecute smugglers.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: They crossed the world to reach the US. Now deported under Trump, they're stuck in Panama
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — They crossed oceans to get to the U.S., fleeing conflict, religious persecution, poverty and government crackdowns in countries such as Afghanistan, Somalia, Cameroon, China, Pakistan and Iran.
item.dc:creator: MATÍAS DELACROIX and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Columbia grad student's detention will stretch on as lawyers spar over Trump's plan to deport him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mahmoud Khalil will remain detained in Louisiana until at least next week but can finally speak to lawyers while they fight the Trump administration’s plans to deport the Columbia University graduate student for his role in campus protests against Israel, a judge decided at a hearing
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Delphi murders: Full 'Bridge Guy' video releases on Richard Allen innocence website
item.description: (WXIN/WTTV) — A full, 43-second video taken by Libby German just before she and her friend Abby Williams were murdered has released online. The video can be seen above.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, NewsNation

item.title: A breakdown of major EPA deregulatory moves around water, air, climate
item.description: Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Lee Zeldin on Wednesday announced nearly three dozen deregulatory moves that he said would spur the U.S. economy by rolling back rules that have unfairly burdened industry.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis, Alexa St. John And Jack Brook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration resumes detention of immigrant families after Biden-era pause
item.description: The Trump administration has resumed family detention of immigrants in a South Texas facility after a Biden-era pause.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Gonzalez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Affordable housing threatened as Trump halts $1 billion slated for extending life of aging buildings
item.description: The Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing, threatening projects that keep tens of thousands of units livable for low-income Americans, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge appears skeptical probationary firings were for performance
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge repeatedly sounded skeptical on Wednesday that the Trump administration’s mass firings of probationary federal workers were made by the government because the employees couldn’t do their jobs, saying the terminations appeared to be part of a larger goal. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing American in Dominican Republic: Unpredictable ocean currents, beachfront pose major search challenge
item.description: The Dominican Republic beach where UPitt student Sudkisha Konanki vanished last week is known for unpredictable currents and is located near the end of a protective coral reef.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Where is Kelly Clarkson? Fans concerned
item.description: Kelly Clarkson's rep didn't return NewsNation's emails, but sources suggest she could be overwhelmed.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana man accused of beating, killing 5-year-old after abuse
item.description: Court documents paint a harrowing picture of continuous alleged abuse of the boy by Brice Bainum, 32.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, NewsNation

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX Dragon launch to relieve stranded astronauts is delayed again
item.description: Less than an hour before liftoff, the SpaceX Dragon mission to provide assistance to two astronauts was aborted yet again, this time for a ground equipment issue.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Virginia track athlete facing assault charge after baton strike she said was accidental
item.description: Alaila Everett said it was an accident but court records show she is now facing a potential assault and battery charge.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KRON4

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: Those who are over the age of 14 will also need to apply for fingerprinting with authorities, according to the mandate uploaded Wednesday to the Federal Register.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Anonymous donor paying to spay, neuter 120 pit bulls in Denver
item.description: "What we're trying to do is just make sure that everybody's got equal access to the resources they need to do right by their pet.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Fierro, NewsNation

item.title: T-Mobile customers to get payments up to $25K next month after data breach: Here's who qualifies
item.description: The $350 million T-Mobile agreed to pay in a 2022 class action settlement will finally start going out to customers next month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Panera unveils green bread bowl for St. Patrick’s Day in select cities
item.description: Panera Bread is turning one of its signature items green in honor of St. Patrick’s Day. The fast-casual chain will launch its limited-edition Pot of Gold, a green sourdough bread bowl filled with its signature Mac and Cheese, on March 16 and 17.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration drops lawsuit against company accused of abusing children at migrant shelters
item.description: The Trump administration says it is dropping a civil lawsuit against the largest provider of housing for unaccompanied migrant children over alleged sexual abuse of minors at its facilities.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Gonzalez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Third suspect in Southern University student's hazing death arrested
item.description: Autopsy results for Caleb Wilson have not yet been released as the coroner's office awaits findings from additional testing.
item.dc:creator: Allison Bruhl, NewsNation

item.title: Passenger attacked flight attendant, swallowed rosary beads on plane to Miami: court docs
item.description: His sister told the FBI agent that they were traveling to Haiti “to flee religious attacks of a spiritual nature.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: In-N-Out CEO Lynsi Snyder says her company remains a family despite ‘betrayal’ from colleagues
item.description: Lynsi Snyder inherited the title of CEO of In-N-Out Burger, and as the only living member of the founding family, she continues to run the ever-growing business like her own family.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: LA woman reunited with cat presumed lost in devastating Palisades Fire
item.description: A California woman who lost her home in the devastating firestorm that engulfed the Palisades experienced a tearful reunion over the weekend with her beloved cat, lost in the charred remains of the neighborhood for some two months.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: The US is short nearly 4 million homes
item.description: Builders faced barriers like zoning and supply costs, which make producing affordable homes challenging.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: Texas execution of 'desert killer,' on death row for 30 years, blocked by court
item.description: David Wood, 67, was sentenced to death in 1992 for the killing of six woman and girls in El Paso, Texas. He has spent nearly 33 years on death row for the crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The EU retaliates against Trump's trade moves and slaps tariffs on produce from Republican states
item.description: The European Union on Wednesday announced retaliatory trade action with new duties on U.S. industrial and farm products, responding within hours to the Trump administration's increase in tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25%.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and DAVID McHUGH Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas measles outbreak rises to 223 cases
item.description: The measles outbreak in the South Plains region of Texas has grown to 223 cases. 29 of those infected have been hospitalized and one school-aged child has died, according to a new update from the Texas Department of State Health Services.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stark, KRON4

item.title: FBI joins search for US college student who vanished in Dominican Republic on spring break
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The FBI has joined the search for a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student who went missing nearly a week ago while on spring break in the Dominican Republic tourist town of Punta Cana, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: 15 injured, 1 seriously, when school bus overturns in New Jersey: officials
item.description: At least 14 juveniles were injured when a school bus overturned on the Garden State Parkway in New Jersey on Monday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: 3 victims of Pennsylvania plane crash were taken to a burn unit for treatment, hospital says
item.description: LITITZ, Pa. (AP) — Three of the five family members injured in a single-engine plane crash in Pennsylvania after the pilot reported an open door on the aircraft were sent to a burn center for treatment, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Statements man charged in Atlanta spa killings made post-arrest can be used at trial, judge rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge on Monday rejected an attempt by attorneys for a man accused of killing four women at Atlanta massage businesses to keep a jury from hearing statements he made after his arrest.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4
